de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.scripts

Interface ProofScriptCommand<T>

  • Type Parameters:
    T - the arguments
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    AbstractCommand, ActivateCommand, AssertCommand, AssumeCommand, AutoCommand, AxiomCommand, CutCommand, EchoCommand, ExitCommand, FocusOnSelectionAndHideCommand, InstantiateCommand, JavascriptCommand, LeaveCommand, LetCommand, MacroCommand, NoArgumentCommand, RewriteCommand, RuleCommand, SaveInstCommand, SaveNewNameCommand, SchemaVarCommand, ScriptCommand, SelectCommand, SetCommand, SetEchoCommand, SetFailOnClosedCommand, SkipCommand, SMTCommand, TryCloseCommand

    public interface ProofScriptCommand<T>
    A ProofScriptCommand is an executable mutation on the given proof. It abstracts complex operations, and made them accessible for an API.

    ProofScriptCommand are supported by the java.util.ServiceLoader. You can add new proof script commands by add a new entry to META-INF/service/de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.scripts.ProofScriptCommand.

    Version 2 (2017-03-28): change of the interface support for structured arguments.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods

      java.lang.String getName()

      • execute

        void execute(AbstractUserInterfaceControl uiControl,
             T args,
             EngineState stateMap)
      throws ScriptException,
             java.lang.InterruptedException
        Parameters:
        uiControl - the current ui controller
        args - the script arguments
        stateMap - the current state
        Throws:
        ScriptException - if something bad happens
        java.lang.InterruptedException - if something bad happens

      • getName

        java.lang.String getName()
        Returns the name of this proof command. The name should be constant and not be clash with the name of other commands. The name is essential for finding this command within an hashmap.
        Returns:
        a non-null, non-empty string
        See Also:
        ProofScriptEngine

      • getDocumentation

        java.lang.String getDocumentation()
        A documentation for the commands.
        Returns:
        a non-null string
